mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates eliminate short-term discount rate pressure and signal central bank liquidity provision, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding one percentage point confirms deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns exceed zero in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized contraction across consumer discretionary and housing demand channels, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive IC over single-series models by >0.01.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated financial stress, a monthly decline in the industrial production index accelerates corporate earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations during stress periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when market volatility exceeds historical 90th percentile.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding two percent confirms sustained economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2%, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a declining federal funds rate and falling monthly inflation indicates a disinflationary easing cycle that improves real discount rates and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month payroll employment decline exceeding two hundred thousand workers signals severe labor market deterioration and impending corporate margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns exceed zero in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal fails to achieve a positive Rank IC in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the industrial production index reflect weakening manufacturing output and supply chain contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations following industrial production declines, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample tests.
